items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.description: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Africa: Our Ocean Conference Ends with Familiar Question - Will Pledges Be Met?
items.description: [allAfrica] Mombasa, Kenya -- A new round of pledges and partnerships marked the closing ceremony of the 11th Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa. Countries and stakeholders outlined commitments to scale up ocean action, finance and innovation. The question, however, remains whether these will amount to yet another list of promises.

items.title: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi had assured him of the safe departure of an Italian ship, the Grimaldi Group's car carrier Grande Torino, that has been blocked in the Persian Gulf for months. Araghchi, said Tajani on X, reporting on a phone call with his counterpart, "assured me that in the next few hours the Iranian authorities will ensure the conditions for the safe departure of the Italian Grimaldi ship 'Grande Torino,'" "blocked for months in the Gulf." (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship

items.title: Sudan: The European Union Delegation in Sudan - a New Political Engagement or an Attempt to Exert Pressure From Within?
items.description: [Dabanga] Amsterdam / Khartoum / Port Sudan -- A high-level delegation of heads of European Union missions, comprising 14 diplomats and headed by the head of the mission to Sudan, Wolfram Vetter, concluded a visit to Sudan, the first of its kind since the outbreak of war on April 15, 2023. The two-day visit to Khartoum and Port Sudan included a series of meetings with Sudanese officials, including the Chairman of the Sovereignty Council, his deputy, the Prime Minister, and other officials.
